agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A new 'auto-split' feature is requested that would automatically divide shipments exceeding 500 kg into multiple sub-shipments, each assigned to a different carrier, bypassing supervisor involvement. This directly conflicts with two established production invariants in the existing domain model.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of goods to be transported, which must have exactly one assigned Carrier at all times."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The entity responsible for transporting a Shipment."},{"term":"Sub-shipment","definition":"A proposed new concept — a child Shipment created by splitting a parent Shipment that exceeds a weight threshold. Not yet part of the domain model."},{"term":"Auto-split","definition":"A proposed automatic process that divides an oversized Shipment into multiple sub-shipments, each with a different Carrier, without Supervisor approval."},{"term":"Supervisor","definition":"The only role authorized to reassign a Carrier on a Shipment after initial assignment."},{"term":"Carrier Assignment Lock","definition":"The invariant that once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can change or reassign it."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint — every Shipment must be traceable to exactly one responsible Carrier. Violating this means accountability is lost and dispatch cannot proceed.","testable_assertion":"At any point in the Shipment lifecycle, querying a Shipment's assigned Carriers returns exactly one."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can reassign it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is an authorization integrity constraint — it prevents unauthorized or automated processes from silently changing carrier responsibility, which would break chain-of-custody guarantees.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to change Carrier assignment without Supervisor authorization is rejected by the system."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"At dispatch time, every Shipment must have exactly one Carrier assigned.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — a shipment on a truck must have one accountable carrier. Without this, dispatch is undefined.","testable_assertion":"The dispatch operation fails if the Shipment does not have exactly one assigned Carrier."}],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"A shipment is assigned to Carrier A, then auto-split triggers, creating sub-shipments with Carriers B and C. The original Carrier A assignment was effectively revoked without Supervisor approval.","expected_behavior":"This must not happen under the current domain model. The auto-split feature as described would silently violate the Carrier Assignment Lock invariant."},{"scenario":"Auto-split creates sub-shipments but the parent shipment retains its original carrier. Now the parent and children both exist — does the parent still represent a real shipment?","expected_behavior":"Undefined. The domain has no concept of parent/child shipment relationships. Introducing this without redefining the aggregate boundary creates an inconsistent model."},{"scenario":"A shipment weighing 501 kg is split into two sub-shipments. One sub-shipment fails carrier assignment. The system now has a sub-shipment with no carrier.","expected_behavior":"Violates BR-1. The invariant that every shipment has exactly one carrier would be broken for the unassigned sub-shipment."},{"scenario":"A shipment is split, and a customer queries tracking. Which shipment ID do they track?","expected_behavior":"Undefined. The domain model has no concept of a shipment being multiple shipments. Customer-facing contracts break."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"If implemented as described, accountability gaps between carriers on split shipments could lead to disputed liability for lost or damaged goods — direct financial exposure.","operational":"Bypassing supervisor oversight on carrier reassignment removes a human checkpoint that exists for a reason. Operational errors in carrier selection would go unchecked. The auto-split also introduces a new aggregate relationship (parent/child shipments) that every downstream system (tracking, billing, dispatch, reporting) must understand.","user":"Customers expect one shipment = one tracking number = one carrier. Splitting silently breaks this mental model and degrades trust.","risk":"HIGH. Two production invariants are directly violated. Shipping this as described would corrupt the domain model and could cause dispatch failures, billing errors, and lost accountability.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Operations / Supervisors","impact":"Their gatekeeper authority over carrier reassignment is bypassed, removing a critical control."},{"group":"Carriers","impact":"Ambiguity over who is responsible for what portion of a split shipment. Liability and SLA disputes."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"A single shipment suddenly becomes multiple sub-shipments with different carriers and potentially different delivery times. Tracking and communication complexity increases."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Billing is currently 1:1 shipment-to-carrier. Split shipments require a new billing model."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"The Shipment aggregate boundary must be redesigned. Every system that assumes one-shipment-one-carrier must be updated."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct contradiction between the requested feature and two established production invariants. The auto-split feature as described is incompatible with the existing domain model. Confidence is low because the feature cannot be implemented as specified without either violating invariants or fundamentally redefining the domain model — and the spec does not acknowledge or resolve this conflict.","high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction between auto-split and the carrier assignment lock invariant is unambiguous","The contradiction between multiple carriers per split shipment and the one-carrier-per-shipment invariant is unambiguous"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the product team intends sub-shipments to be first-class Shipments or a new entity type","Whether the 500 kg threshold is a hard physical constraint or a soft policy","Whether supervisor bypass is intentional or an oversight in the feature request"],"assumptions":["The existing invariants are correctly stated and are enforced in production","Sub-shipments would be treated as Shipments within the existing domain model"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"The auto-split feature directly violates two production invariants: (1) A Shipment must have exactly one Carrier — auto-split creates multiple sub-shipments each with different carriers, meaning the original shipment's one-carrier guarantee is broken. (2) Only a Supervisor can reassign a Carrier — auto-split reassigns carrier responsibility automatically without supervisor involvement. These are not soft policies; they are invariants that the system currently guarantees.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations / Supervisors","Carriers","Customers","Finance / Billing","Engineering"],"options":["A: Reject the feature as specified. The invariants must hold.","B: Redefine the domain model. Introduce a new 'ShipmentGroup' or 'MultiCarrierShipment' aggregate that is distinct from Shipment. Each sub-shipment remains a proper Shipment with exactly one Carrier. Supervisor approval is required to initiate the split. This preserves both invariants while enabling the business capability.","C: Relax the invariants. Formally downgrade the one-carrier and supervisor-lock rules to policies. This is a major domain model change that affects every downstream system and must be a deliberate business decision, not a side effect of a feature."],"recommendation":"Option B. Introduce a new aggregate (e.g., ShipmentGroup) that composes multiple Shipments. Each sub-shipment is a full Shipment with one Carrier. The split operation requires Supervisor authorization. The 500 kg threshold triggers a recommendation to split, but a Supervisor must approve it. This preserves all invariants while delivering the business value of handling oversized shipments."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Auto-split assigns multiple carriers across sub-shipments without maintaining the one-carrier-per-shipment invariant on the original Shipment entity.","business_rule_broken":"BR-1: A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier."},{"violation":"Auto-split reassigns carrier responsibility automatically, bypassing the Supervisor authorization gate.","business_rule_broken":"BR-2: Once a Carrier is assigned, only a Supervisor can reassign."}]}
